name: yargy

metadata.name: yargy
metadata.summary: Rule-based facts extraction for Russian language

documentation: ![CI](https://github.com/natasha/yargy/actions/workflows/test.yml/badge.svg) Yargy uses rules and dictionaries to extract structured information from Russian texts. Yargy is similar to Tomita parser. ## Install Yargy supports Python 3.7+, PyPy 3, depends only on Pymorphy2. ```bash $ pip install yargy ``` ## Usage ```python from yargy import Parser, rule, and_, not_ from yargy.interpretation import fact from yargy.predicates import gram from yargy.relations import gnc_relation from yargy.pipelines import morph_pipeline Name = fact( 'Name', ['first', 'last'], ) Person = fact( 'Person', ['position', 'name'] ) LAST = and_( gram('Surn'), not_(gram('Abbr')), ) FIRST = and_( gram('Name'), not_(gram('Abbr')), ) POSITION = morph_pipeline([ 'управляющий директор', 'вице-мэр' ]) gnc = gnc_relation() NAME = rule( FIRST.interpretation( Name.first ).match(gnc), LAST.interpretation( Name.last ).match(gnc) ).interpretation( Name ) PERSON = rule( POSITION.interpretation( Person.position ).match(gnc), NAME.interpretation( Person.name ) ).interpretation( Person ) parser = Parser(PERSON) match = parser.match('управляющий директор Иван Ульянов') print(match) Person( position='управляющий директор', name=Name( first='Иван', last='Ульянов' ) ) ``` ## Documentation All materials are in Russian: * Overview * Video from workshop * Getting started * Reference * Cookbook * Examples * Code snippets ## Support - Chat — https://t.me/natural_language_processing - Issues — https://github.com/natasha/yargy/issues - Commercial support — https://lab.alexkuk.ru ## Development Dev env ```bash brew install graphviz python -m venv ~/.venvs/natasha-yargy source ~/.venvs/natasha-yargy/bin/activate pip install -r requirements/dev.txt pip install -e . python -m ipykernel install --user --name natasha-yargy ``` Test + lint ```bash make test ``` Update docs ```bash make exec-docs # Manually check git diff docs/, commit ``` Release ```bash # Update setup.py version git commit -am 'Up version' git tag v0.16.0 git push git push --tags # Github Action builds dist and publishes to PyPi ```

classes.name: yargy.predicates.bank.true
documentation:
Всегда возвращает True

```pycon
>>> predicate = true()
>>> predicate(False)
True
```

classes.name: yargy.predicates.bank.is_lower
documentation:
str.islower

```pycon
>>> predicate = is_lower()
>>> a, b = tokenize('xxx Xxx')
>>> predicate(a)
True
>>> predicate(b)
False
```

classes.name: yargy.predicates.bank.is_upper
documentation:
str.isupper

```pycon
>>> predicate = is_upper()
>>> a, b = tokenize('XXX xxx')
>>> predicate(a)
True
>>> predicate(b)
False
```

classes.name: yargy.predicates.bank.is_title
documentation:
str.istitle

```pycon
>>> predicate = is_title()
>>> a, b = tokenize('XXX Xxx')
>>> predicate(a)
False
>>> predicate(b)
True
```

classes.name: yargy.predicates.bank.is_capitalized
documentation:
Слово написано с большой буквы

```pycon
>>> predicate = is_capitalized()
>>> a, b, c = tokenize('Xxx XXX xxX')
>>> predicate(a)
True
>>> predicate(b)
True
>>> predicate(c)
False
```

classes.name: yargy.predicates.bank.eq
documentation:
a == b

```pycon
>>> predicate = eq('1')
>>> token, = tokenize('1')
>>> predicate(token)
True
```

classes.name: yargy.predicates.bank.length_eq
documentation:
len(a) == b

```pycon
>>> predicate = length_eq(3)
>>> a, b = tokenize('XXX 123')
>>> predicate(a)
True
>>> predicate(b)
True
```

classes.name: yargy.predicates.bank.gte
documentation:
a >= b

```pycon
>>> predicate = gte(4)
>>> a, b, c = tokenize('3 5 C')
>>> predicate(a)
False
>>> predicate(b)
True
>>> predicate(c)
False
```

classes.name: yargy.predicates.bank.lte
documentation:
a \<= b

```pycon
>>> predicate = lte(4)
>>> a, b, c = tokenize('3 5 C')
>>> predicate(a)
True
>>> predicate(b)
False
>>> predicate(c)
False
```

classes.name: yargy.predicates.bank.caseless
documentation:
a.lower() == b.lower()

```pycon
>>> predicate = caseless('Рано')
>>> token, = tokenize('РАНО')
>>> predicate(token)
True
```

classes.name: yargy.predicates.bank.in_
documentation:
a in b

```pycon
>>> predicate = in_({'S', 'M', 'L'})
>>> a, b = tokenize('S 1')
>>> predicate(a)
True
>>> predicate(b)
False
```

classes.name: yargy.predicates.bank.in_caseless
documentation:
a.lower() in b

```pycon
>>> predicate = in_caseless({'S', 'M', 'L'})
>>> a, b = tokenize('S m')
>>> predicate(a)
True
>>> predicate(b)
True
```

classes.name: yargy.predicates.bank.normalized
documentation:
Нормальная форма слова == value

```pycon
>>> a = activate(normalized('сталь'))
>>> b = activate(normalized('стать'))
>>> token, = tokenize('стали')
>>> a(token)
True
>>> b(token)
True
```

classes.name: yargy.predicates.bank.dictionary
documentation:
Нормальная форма слова in value

```pycon
>>> predicate = activate(dictionary({'учитель', 'врач'}))
>>> a, b = tokenize('учителя врачи')
>>> predicate(a)
True
>>> predicate(b)
True
```

classes.name: yargy.predicates.bank.gram
documentation:
value есть среди граммем слова

```pycon
>>> a = activate(gram('NOUN'))
>>> b = activate(gram('VERB'))
>>> token, = tokenize('стали')
>>> a(token)
True
>>> b(token)
True
```

classes.name: yargy.predicates.bank.type
documentation:
Тип токена равен value

```pycon
>>> predicate = activate(type('INT'))
>>> a, b = tokenize('3 раза')
>>> predicate(a)
True
>>> predicate(b)
False
```

classes.name: yargy.predicates.bank.tag
documentation: Тег токена равен value

classes.name: yargy.predicates.bank.is_single
documentation:
Слово в единственном числе

```pycon
>>> predicate = is_single()
>>> token, = tokenize('слово')
>>> predicate(token)
True
```

classes.name: yargy.predicates.bank.custom
documentation:
function в качестве предиката

```pycon
>>> from math import log
>>> f = lambda x: int(log(int(x), 10)) == 2
>>> predicate = activate(custom(f, types=INT))
>>> a, b = tokenize('12 123')
>>> predicate(a)
False
>>> predicate(b)
True
```
